summary: A stable low-friction lane for sliding a puck/stone/object into a scoring or target region.
constraints: The sliding object must be passively stable before contact; low friction does not mean unsupported or falling.
Use a stable floor/ice lane under the object and keep passive drift <= 8 px before agent contact.
Place agent -> object -> scoring region roughly collinear.
Keep the first slide short enough for validation, preferably object-to-region <= 180 px unless the lane is strongly guided.
Use a larger non-blocking sensor region for scoring circles so check_objective matches the visible target.
Avoid static floor geometry being treated as a blocker between object and region by keeping the sliding lane below the object and not crossing the target sensor volume.
repair_guidance: If the stone drifts before contact, add a stable ice lane/floor under it rather than removing friction entirely.
If the slide overshoots or misses, enlarge the scoring sensor or add subtle guide rails.
If generic push validation is too strict, keep the object closer or make the objective progress tier honest for long-range sliding.
